ocr.text: The box office performance of the media and entertainment sector in the Asia-Pacific region has seen significant fluctuations
over the past few years, influenced by various cultural, economic, and technological factors. In particular, the analysis of the
year-on-year (YoY) change in box office revenue presents insights into the industry's recovery trajectory following the
challenges posed by the global pandemic. For instance, in 2021, the YoY change was recorded at 12.5%, indicating a positive
rebound from the previous year's downturn. This resurgence can be attributed to the gradual reopening of cinemas and the
release of highly anticipated films that drew audiences back to theaters. Furthermore, the table also illustrates the market share
dynamics across different players in the industry, with one notable figure being Sony Group's impressive market share, which
reached a substantial 150B JPY. This figure reflects not only the company's strategic positioning but also its ability to adapt to

shifting consumer preferences in an increasingly competitive landscape.

As the region continues to evolve, several factors will
play a critical role in shaping future box office performance. The integration of advanced technologies, such as augmented
reality (AR) and virtual reality (VR), into the cinematic experience is expected to enhance viewer engagement and drive ticket
sales. Additionally, the rise of streaming platforms has transformed audience viewing habits, compelling traditional cinema
operators to innovate in order to retain their market share.

The interplay between these emerging trends and the established cinematic offerings will likely dictate the sector's growth

trajectory in the coming years.

Moreover, insights from the box office performance data reveal differing impacts across various
markets within the Asia-Pacific region. Countries with more robust infrastructure and a higher density of urban populations tend
to exhibit stronger box office revenues. For instance, the 2020 figures highlight substantial declines in box office performance
due to pandemic-related restrictions, underscoring the volatility of the market. As the industry adjusts to the new normal,

understanding these shifts will be vital for stakeholders aiming to maximize their contributions to the sector.

In conclusion, the
analysis encapsulated within this report delineates the current box office performance landscape in the Asia-Pacific region,
emphasizing key metrics such as YoY change and market share. As demonstrated, Sony Group's strategic initiatives have
positioned it favorably, with a market share of 150B JPY, while the YoY change of 12.5% in 2021 signals a promising recovery
trajectory. The data presented serves as a crucial touchpoint for industry stakeholders, enabling informed decision-making and
strategic planning as they navigate the complexities of this ever-evolving market. Future performance will depend not only on
the resilience of traditional cinema but also on its ability to innovate and adapt in the face of rapid technological advancements
and shifting consumer preferences.
